  "findings": [
    "Proposed recovery plan violates the mandatory inspection hold constraint.",
    "Starting insulation (J) before rough inspection (I) is signed off covers up rough-in work, preventing code compliance verification."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the proposed recovery plan.",
    "Maintain the mandatory rough inspection hold (I) before starting insulation (J).",
    "Identify alternative recovery options such as crashing non-critical path activities or adding resources to framing or rough-ins."
  ],
  "rationale": "The foreman's proposed recovery plan to start insulation (J) before the rough inspection (I) is signed off directly violates the hard constraint that inspection holds cannot be bypassed. In commercial construction, covering rough-in work (electrical, plumbing, HVAC) with insulation before a third-party or municipal inspection is a major code violation and results in mandatory rework to expose the covered systems.",
  "references": [
    "International Residential Code (IRC) R109.1.4",
    "International Building Code (IBC) 110.3.5"
  ]
}
